summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-encrypted Windows binary from unrelated GitHub repo
details: On `npm install` (Windows), the package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js:5-6) executes a base64-encoded UTF-16LE PowerShell payload via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <...>`. The decoded payload downloads `launcher.bin` from `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin` — a personal GitHub repository unrelated to the `mailconfirmer` package — XOR-decrypts the bytes with key 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, launches it hidden via `Start-Process`, then removes the file. After dropping the binary, the script tampers with the parent project's `package.json` and `package-lock.json` to delete every `mailconfirmer` entry from dependencies/devDependencies and lockfile packages (install-hook.js:24-27), and schedules a hidden PowerShell job that sleeps 30 seconds and then recursively removes the package's own `node_modules` directory (install-hook.js:69-78). The advertised library surface is a stub: `index.js` exports `sendConfirmationEmail` and `verifyToken` functions that only `console.log` and resolve, so the package has no legitimate purpose — it exists solely as a delivery vehicle for the dropper. The combination of opaque attacker-controlled binary execution at install time, mismatched publisher, anti-forensic manifest tampering, and self-deletion is unambiguously malicious.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
